_meta.name: Proprietary Therapeutic Frameworks
_meta.description: Mind the Box original methodologies and frameworks for therapeutic content creation

wardrobe.name: The Wardrobe Framework
wardrobe.description: A therapeutic approach using the metaphor of a wardrobe to help people understand emotional patterns and coping mechanisms

core_reframe.truth: What people call 'comfort zone' is actually a 'controlled zone' - a small cramped space where they experience familiar, predictable pain
core_reframe.insight: They stay not because it's comfortable, but because the pain is controllable

wardrobe.why_people_stay: The pain is controllable, not because it's comfortable

room.why_it_feels_unsafe: Trauma once entered uninvited, so the space feels dangerous

trauma.rule: Stays outside. You go to it when ready - it doesn't get invited in
trauma.boundary: I will meet trauma where it is, I won't invite it in
trauma.key_insight: The room doesn't disappear when you leave it. It's there, waiting, full of everything you put in it.

sequence.action: Learning you can protect the room
Opening the wardrobe door when ready

sequence.meaning: So it feels full, not empty and frightening

sequence.action: Building a space so safe and joyful
sequence.meaning: You don't need the wardrobe anymore

healing_process.what_this_is: Pre-therapy, alongside-therapy, or instead-of-therapy-for-now. Building capacity to eventually do deeper work if you choose.
healing_process.what_this_is_not: Processing trauma from inside the wardrobe. That's retraumatising, not healing.

joy.question: Does this bring genuine pleasure/fulfilment?
joy.role: Compass for expansion - growth happens as side effect of reaching toward joy
joy.application: If it doesn't feel joyful, adjust until it does, but keep safety and values

safety.question: Do I feel secure and regulated?
safety.application: If it doesn't feel safe, make it smaller until it does, but keep joy and values

values.question: Does this align with what matters to me? Does it increase my capacity to live my values?
values.role: Purpose - ensures choices aren't just distraction but build who you want to be
values.application: If it doesn't connect to values, find the link, or adjust until it builds who you want to be

no_compromise_principle.description: Joy, safety, and values go together. Never choose between them.
no_compromise_principle.core_insight: It's about increasing your capacity to live your values. Setting boundaries increases capacity for honesty. Speaking kindly to yourself increases capacity for kindness to others. Choosing joy increases capacity to show up fully for what matters.

joy_without_safety.example: Excitement that burns out or hurts you

joy_and_safety_without_values.example: Pleasant but empty, doesn't build anything

values_without_joy_or_safety.result: Becomes duty or burns you out

section_flow.description: Each section moves the reader through a cycle of choosing, trying, observing, and choosing again. By the end, they've practised the skill of choosing joy so many times it becomes natural.
section_flow.core_message: It's safe to choose. It's safe to change. It's safe to try.
section_flow.reframe: Real control isn't staying in the controlled zone. Real control is the power to choose.

sections.name: Identify joy
sections.action: Introduce the concept, framed through joy. What would this look like if it brought you joy?
sections.choice: Choose what joy looks like for them

sections.name: Joy + safety + values
sections.action: Find the version that's joyful, safe, AND increases capacity to live values. No compromise.

sections.name: Try
sections.action: Do the thing, notice emotions (joy or not joy, safe or not safe, connected to values or not)

sections.name: Reflect
sections.action: Observe what happened, decide: continue, refine, or choose something different

sections.name: Try again
sections.action: Same thing or something different, getting closer to joy + safety + values

sections.name: Plan for hard moments
sections.action: When it feels scary, what's my joy choice that keeps me safe and builds my values?

sections.name: Apply as real choice
sections.action: Use joy choice in a real moment, all three together
sections.choice: Choose in the moment, when it matters

application_in_content: Use wardrobe metaphor when discussing emotional patterns
Emphasise 'addition not subtraction' in all growth messaging
Frame coping mechanisms as previously useful, now expandable
Every section gives the reader a choice - control through choosing
Never force people out of the wardrobe - invite them to open the door
Always check: is this joyful? Is this safe? Does this build capacity to live values? All three?
key_phrases: Your comfort zone might actually be your controlled zone
Familiar doesn't mean comfortable
Cramped but predictable
The room doesn't disappear when you leave it
You go to trauma when you're ready - it doesn't get invited in
It's safe to choose. It's safe to change. It's safe to try.
Real control is the power to choose

controlled_vs_comfort.name: Controlled Zone vs Comfort Zone
controlled_vs_comfort.description: Reframes the common 'get out of your comfort zone' advice

controlled_zone.actual_nature: Predictable, known, but often cramped and limiting

comfort_zone.actual_nature: Regulated nervous system, genuine ease, expansion

controlled_vs_comfort.key_insight: We mistake familiarity for comfort. Familiar doesn't mean comfortable.
application: Use when encouraging growth without shaming current patterns
Helps explain why change is hard even when people want it
key_phrases: Your comfort zone might actually be your controlled zone
Familiar doesn't mean comfortable
Cramped but predictable
Known pain feels safer than unknown possibility

addition_not_subtraction.name: Addition Not Subtraction
addition_not_subtraction.description: Core principle that growth comes from adding capacity, not removing coping mechanisms

principle.statement: Never take away without offering something better
principle.rationale: Coping mechanisms exist for a reason. They kept you safe. They're not wrong - they're just not the only option anymore.
principle.application: Build a bigger wardrobe. More outfits for more situations. The old ones don't have to go.

in_practice.right_approach: What if you could also do Y? (expanding, inviting, adding)

examples.addition: What else could you reach for when you're stressed? The food can stay as an option.
What would it feel like to have 'maybe' or 'let me think about it' as options too?
What would make having that conversation feel safer? Let's build that first.

addition_not_subtraction.connection_to_wardrobe: Just as a wardrobe contains different clothes for different occasions, we develop different emotional 'outfits' for different situations. You can add new outfits without throwing out old ones.
key_phrases: Addition, not subtraction
You can add new options without removing old ones
Your coping mechanisms aren't wrong - they're just not the only option
Build capacity, don't force change
The old ways can stay. We're just adding more.

choice_as_safety.name: Choice as Safety
choice_as_safety.description: The understanding that having choice creates safety, and safety enables choice

principle.statement: Control through choosing, not through staying still
principle.core_message: It's safe to choose. It's safe to change. It's safe to try.
principle.reframe: Real control isn't staying in the controlled zone. Real control is the power to choose - what enters your room, what you reach toward, what you do when things feel hard.

application: Every section gives the reader a choice
Frame exercises as invitations, not instructions
Honour 'not yet' as a valid choice
key_phrases: It's safe to choose
It's safe to change
It's safe to try
You get to decide
This is an invitation, not an instruction

no_failure_only_signals.name: No Failure, Only Signals
no_failure_only_signals.description: Reframes setbacks as information rather than evidence of failure

principle.statement: Old patterns returning = need more of what you were giving yourself
principle.rationale: If a coping mechanism resurfaces, it's because you needed what it provides. That's not failure - that's your system communicating.
principle.application: Instead of 'I failed', ask 'What was I needing? How else could I get that?'

examples.pattern: Returned to emotional eating after weeks of not doing it
examples.old_frame: I failed. I'm back to square one.
examples.new_frame: I needed comfort and my system reached for what it knows. What else could provide that comfort?

examples.pattern: Snapped at partner after practising calm communication
examples.old_frame: I can't do this. I'll never change.
examples.new_frame: I was dysregulated. What happened before that? What did I need?

key_phrases: No failure, only signals
What were you needing?
Your system is communicating, not failing
This is information, not evidence against you

resilience_before_processing.name: Resilience Before Processing
resilience_before_processing.description: The principle that capacity must be built before trauma can be safely approached

principle.statement: Don't process trauma from inside the wardrobe. Build the room first.
principle.rationale: Processing trauma requires a safe base to return to. Without the room, there's nowhere to land.
principle.sequence: Safety → Capacity → Choice to approach (if and when ready)

what_this_means.goal: Building the capacity to eventually do deeper work if you choose to

trauma_boundary.rule: Trauma stays outside. You go to it when you're ready.
trauma_boundary.how: On neutral ground. With support. On your terms.
trauma_boundary.key_insight: When you're strong enough, standing in your room, feeling solid - you can choose to step outside and meet it. That's not avoidance. That's sovereignty.

key_phrases: Build the room first
Resilience before processing
You go to trauma when you're ready - it doesn't come to you
That's not avoidance. That's sovereignty.

small_steps_toward_joy.name: Small Steps Toward Joy
small_steps_toward_joy.description: Growth through reaching toward joy rather than pushing through fear

principle.statement: Expand by reaching toward joy, not by forcing yourself through fear

contrast.traditional: Push through your comfort zone. Do the scary thing.
contrast.this_approach: What's one small thing that would bring joy? Start there.

mechanism.how_it_works: Each step toward joy is an invitation, not a challenge. Growth happens as a side effect.
mechanism.why_it_works: Joy is sustainable motivation. Fear-based pushing leads to burnout and rebound.

key_phrases: Small steps toward joy
Each step an invitation, not a challenge
Growth happens as a side effect of reaching toward joy
What's one small thing that would bring joy?

always: Frame coping mechanisms as previously useful, now expandable
Give the reader choice in every section
Check for joy + safety + values alignment
Use addition language, not subtraction language
never: Force people out of the wardrobe
Suggest processing trauma without safety first

instead_of.use: What if you could also do Y?
You might find it helpful to

instead_of.avoid: Get out of your comfort zone
instead_of.use: What would feel like a stretch but still safe?
